step_by_step_thinking:
I largely concur with the decision but would like to add another aspect that reinforces why option D (3600) is correct. Given a 20% relative precision target, achieving such tight confidence intervals necessitates larger samples even if the baseline prevalence is not extremely low. Additionally, maintaining power close to just over half (20%) requires substantial numbers to reduce Type II errors meaningfully. Thus, while the other factors certainly play into it, ensuring sufficient statistical power under strict criteria is crucial here.
```plaintext
I agree with the selection of D: 3600.
